attractions.name: Kharpandi Goemba
attractions.description: Religious shrine situated 400 meters above an astonishing valley surrounded by coniferous trees, believed to bless infertile couples with children

attractions.name: Buddha Dordenma (Buddha View Point)
attractions.description: 169 feet bronze statue of Buddha overlooking the valley of Thimphu, one of the largest Buddha statues in the world built to fulfill prophecy dating back to 8th century

attractions.name: Simply Bhutan
attractions.description: Interactive 'living' museum that gives a quick introduction to various aspects of traditional life in Bhutan with mocked-up village scenes

attractions.name: Craft Bazar
attractions.description: Line of bamboo huts showcasing a wide range of authentic Bhutan-made arts and crafts products with reasonable price

attractions.name: Tashichhodzong
attractions.description: Largest fortress in Bhutan, seat of government since 1968, houses throne room and offices of the king, cabinet secretariat and ministries

attractions.name: Motithang Takin Preserve
attractions.description: Home to Takin, the national animal of Bhutan and one of the strangest looking creatures in the world

attractions.name: National Memorial Chorten
attractions.description: Most visible religious landmark in Bhutan, built in 1974 in memory of third king Jigme Dorji Wangchuck

attractions.name: National Institute of Zorig Chusum (Art and Crafts Center)
attractions.description: Institute providing vocational training on Bhutanese Arts and Crafts where visitors can witness students training

local_dishes.name: Arra
local_dishes.description: Local rice spirit served as greeting at Simply Bhutan museum

souvenirs.item: Authentic Bhutan-made arts and crafts products
